response_raw:
## 1. Rider objective & context

The footage is a first-person (helmet/chest-mounted) POV clip recorded on a DJI Osmo Action 5 Pro (watermarked "2026"). The motorcycle — visibly marked as a **GASGAS** with **Renthal** handlebars — is being ridden along off-road trails and through undeveloped woodland terrain. The sequence begins with an exterior view of the trail before transitioning to a POV riding perspective.

---

## 2. Rider

- The rider is **not directly visible** in any frame; the camera is mounted on the rider or bike, providing a POV perspective.
- In later frames (frames 12–16), a **gloved hand** is briefly visible at the bottom-right of frame, suggesting the rider is present on the ground near the fallen motorcycle.
- Rider body position, gear, and posture are **not visible**.

---

## 3. Terrain & environment

- **Deciduous woodland** with bare trees, consistent with late autumn or winter conditions.
- Ground is covered in **dry leaf litter**, exposed dirt, rocks, and scattered fallen branches and twigs.
- Terrain is **hilly and uneven**, with visible embankments, gullies, and elevation changes.
- Early frames show a **wider dirt trail/track** with eroded banks and some gravel/rock patches.
- Later frames show progressively **narrower, less defined paths** through dense undergrowth and scrub.
- A section with **multiple fallen/downed trees and branches** crossing the path is visible in frames 11 onward.
- Sky is overcast/grey throughout; lighting is flat and diffuse.
- No other riders, spectators, or man-made structures are visible.

---

## 4. Events

- **Frames 1–2:** Exterior shots of a dirt trail descending into a wooded hollow. No motorcycle visible; camera appears stationary or moving slowly.
- **Frame 3:** POV begins. The red GASGAS motorcycle's front end and handlebars are visible. The bike is moving along a narrow dirt path through the woods.
- **Frame 4:** Camera angle is tilted/low; the red front fender is visible at the bottom of frame. The bike appears to be moving through a flat, leaf-covered section between trees.
- **Frame 5:** Similar POV, bike moving through open flat woodland. Dirt path is faint.
- **Frame 6:** POV continues through denser woodland. A cluster of fallen branches is visible ahead on the left side of the trail.
- **Frame 7:** Camera is now tilted significantly upward/skyward; the front of the bike (top of the fender, handlebars) is visible low in frame, suggesting the bike nose is pointing upward — possible climb or the bike/camera angle has changed abruptly.
- **Frame 8:** Similar steep upward camera angle; bike nose still visible at bottom. Dense scrub ahead.
- **Frame 9:** Camera angle tilted further; now nearly horizontal to the ground. Renthal handlebar pad is prominently visible close to the lens — the camera appears very close to the bars, suggesting a loss of normal riding position.
- **Frame 10:** Handlebar/top of bike visible; camera angle is steep/tilted. Bike appears to be pointing up a heavily shrubbed slope with no clear path. Multiple thin branches and brush visible.
- **Frame 11:** The camera is now oriented sideways/ground-level. The **motorcycle is visibly on its side** on the leaf-covered slope, with the rear wheel, swingarm, and red bodywork visible. Multiple downed branches surround the bike.
- **Frame 12:** Similar ground-level view; motorcycle lying on its left side on the hillside. Front wheel, fork, and red bodywork visible. Downed branches present.
- **Frame 13:** Close-up, low-angle view of the fallen motorcycle. **"GASGAS"** text is legible (inverted) on the side panel. The bodywork shows heavy scratching and paint wear. Renthal handlebars are visible. A **gloved hand** is visible at bottom-right of frame. The bike is stationary on the ground.

---

## 5. Outcome

- The motorcycle is shown **lying on its side** on a leaf-covered wooded hillside, surrounded by downed branches and scrub.
- The bike appears to have **fallen over** during the sequence — the camera transitions from forward-moving POV to a ground-level stationary view consistent with the bike being down.
- The GASGAS bodywork is visibly **heavily worn and scratched** (pre-existing condition observable in earlier frames as well).
- The rider is **not visible standing or moving** in the final frames; only a gloved hand is briefly observed near the downed bike.

---

## 6. Unclear or not visible

- **Rider's full body, helmet, and protective gear** are not visible at any point.
- **Rider's condition** after the motorcycle came to rest on its side is not visible.
- **Speed at the time of the fall** is not determinable from still frames.
- **Exact moment of the fall** is not captured between frames due to interval sampling.
- Whether the motorcycle sustained **mechanical damage** beyond visible cosmetic wear cannot be confirmed.
- Whether any **other riders or persons** were present off-camera is unknown.
- The **specific location/trail name** is not identifiable.
- Engine state (running or stalled) in the final frames is **not visible or audible**.
